title: Week of July 6, 2026: County Road Money, Stillwater's Packet, and Two New Cities
summary:
Going deeper this week: Washington County votes on ~$4 million in road work (July 7); Stillwater's council packet has a new Parks Master Plan, two paving contracts to Miller Excavating, a water-tower painting change order now at ~$642K (tied to the city's PFAS response), and a school resource officer renewal; Lake Elmo holds a closed session on threatened litigation and receives its 2025 audit; and Woodbury weighs the Bailey West rezoning/plat at a public hearing. Plus Oakdale's City Hall rebuild and the Stillwater district's sports-fee and class-size changes.

HOW TO GET INVOLVED
- Washington County Board: Tue, July 7, 9:00 AM, County Board Room, Government Center, Stillwater. Public comment period; fill out a comment card before the meeting.
- Stillwater City Council: Tue, July 7, workshop 4:30 PM / regular 7:00 PM, Stillwater City Hall.
- Lake Elmo City Council: Tue, July 7, closed session 6:00 PM / regular 7:00 PM, 3880 Laverne Ave N.
- Woodbury City Council: Wed, July 8, 7:30 PM, 8301 Valley Creek Rd — includes the Bailey West public hearing.
- Oakdale Planning Commission: Thu, July 9; two public hearings scheduled.
- County online comment windows (washingtoncountymn.gov): proposed Middle St. Croix Valley-to-Phalen-Keller regional trail closes July 10; County Road 10/17 intersection near Lake Elmo closes July 12.
- Stillwater middle-school sports fee: the final amount is set before mid-July registration; watch the district's Community Education page.
- Stuff the Bus school-supply help: register through July 23 at uwwce.org/stuff-the-bus.
- Also coming up: Woodbury 2027 road-reconstruction public hearing July 22; County Board and Oakdale City Council July 14; Stillwater City Council July 21; Stillwater school board July 28.

Hosted by Artie Fishel. Sources: official city, county, and school-district agendas, meeting packets, and staff reports.
